name: Castillo de La Calahorra — circular from Guadix via Jérez de Marquesado
name_original: Castillo de La Calahorra — circular desde Guadix por Jérez de Marquesado
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Guadix traverse a diverse landscape in the province of Granada, Andalusia. The region is characterized by arid badlands, including the unique Gorafe Desert with its eroded clay and sandstone formations. It is framed by significant mountain ranges such as the Sierra Nevada to the south and the Sierra de Baza to the east, offering varied elevation changes. The Hoya de Guadix plain provides a mix of open spaces and fertile zones, alongside distinctive cave dwellings carved into the soft rock. Best touring cycling routes around Guadix The most popular touring cycling route is Castillo de La Calahorra — circular from Guadix via Jérez de Marquesado, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route passes by the historic Castillo de La Calahorra. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Quéntar Reservoir – Genil Riverbank (Granada) loop from Guadix, a difficult 62.1 miles (99.9 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and explores areas around the Quéntar Reservoir and Genil River. Local touring cyclists also love the Gorafe Desert – Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint loop from Cuevas del Ciego, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail leading through the distinctive Gorafe Desert, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Guadix is defined by arid badlands, the imposing Sierra Nevada mountains, and unique cave house settlements. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths through eroded landscapes to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Guadix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Guadix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Guadix?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Guadix listed on komoot. These routes cater to various preferences, from moderate paths through eroded landscapes to challenging mountain climbs, ensuring a diverse experience for cyclists.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Guadix?
A: The routes around Guadix offer a range of difficulties. While there are 5 easy and 15 moderate routes, the majority, 31 routes, are classified as difficult. This means many routes feature significant elevation gains and challenging terrain, often requiring good to very good fitness. E-bikes can make the hilly terrain more accessible.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about the routes around Guadix?
A: The touring cycling routes around Guadix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the surreal badlands and the unique cave houses to the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra Nevada mountains.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes near Guadix?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31896878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quéntar Reservoir – Genil Riverbank (Granada) loop from Guadix&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial challenge, covering nearly 100 km (62 miles) with significant elevation gain. This route explores areas around the Quéntar Reservoir and the Genil River.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Guadix area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Guadix are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042131644/castillo-de-la-calahorra-circular-from-guadix-via-jerez-de-marquesado" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de La Calahorra — circular from Guadix via Jérez de Marquesado&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 44.6 km (27.7 miles) trail passing by the historic Castillo de La Calahorra.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while cycling around Guadix?
A: Guadix offers a wealth of natural and historical sights. You can cycle through the surreal &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt;, known for its eroded clay and sandstone formations. Historic landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadix Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and the Castillo de La Calahorra. Don't miss the unique cave houses, a defining feature of the region.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Guadix?
A: While many routes in Guadix are challenging, there are 5 routes classified as easy, which may be more suitable for families or those cycling with children. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and cover shorter distances, offering a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Guadix?
A: Guadix offers diverse cycling opportunities throughout the year. The cooler temperatures inside the cave houses provide a natural refuge during hotter months. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant weather for cycling, while winter can provide unique experiences with views of the snow-capped Sierra Nevada. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there routes that explore the unique cave houses of Guadix?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its distinctive cave houses. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17237334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe – Cave town Guadix loop from Hernán-Valle&lt;/a&gt; allow you to experience these unique troglodyte dwellings and the surrounding landscapes. Many viewpoints also offer panoramic views of these fascinating settlements.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Guadix is rich in breathtaking viewpoints. While specific routes may vary, the region offers numerous spots to admire the badlands, the Sierra Nevada, and the cave houses. Look for routes that pass through areas like Purullena, Marchal, or offer vistas towards the Sierra Nevada for stunning panoramas.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that go through the Gorafe Desert?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38222128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert – Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint loop from Cuevas del Ciego&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading you directly through the distinctive Gorafe Desert. This area is characterized by its large gullies and colorful sandstone, including the striking red hues of the 'Los Coloraos' area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Guadix?
A: The terrain around Guadix is incredibly varied. You'll encounter everything from winding tarmac roads through mountain ranges like the Sierra Nevada and Sierra de Baza, to gravel paths crisscrossing the arid badlands and the unique Gorafe Desert. The Hoya de Guadix plain also offers a mix of open spaces and fertile zones.
tips: the Barrio de las Cuevas is just outside the center above the pottery district.

Jerez del Marquesado is another of the beautiful towns in the Guadix region. With a rich historical heritage, it invites you to rest from pedaling in its streets, and leaves you wanting to return to explore its tourist attractions in depth.

It is a town with a long history behind it. The castle of La Calahorra, built in the 16th century, dominates with its presence, although the church of Nuestra Señora de la Anunciación, the square and its well-kept houses are well worth a visit.
